title: Cleome dolichocarpa
intro: Cleome dolichocarpa, also known as the Long-stalked Spider Flower, is a plant species native to tropical regions, characterized by its showy, pink or purple flowers and tall, erect growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome dolichocarpa include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Cleome dolichocarpa include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Cleome dolichocarpa is classified within the family Cleomaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as spider flowers. The genus Cleome belongs to the order Brassicales, a large and diverse group of plants that also includes mustard, cabbage, and broccoli. Cleome dolichocarpa is a species of flowering plant native to South America. This species is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cleomoideae within the family Cleomaceae. Cleome dolichocarpa is an annual or perennial plant that can grow up to a certain height, producing small white or purple flowers.
uses: Cleome dolichocarpa, which is also known as the spider flower, has been found to have potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant contains compounds that are reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
